answers.a: A signal that repeats itself exactly after a fixed time period
answers.c: A signal that only contains sine waves
answers.d: A signal that only exists for a finite time

explanations.a: Correct! A periodic signal is one that repeats its values at regular intervals or periods
explanations.b: Incorrect. This describes an aperiodic signal
explanations.c: Incorrect. Periodic signals can contain any waveform that repeats
explanations.d: Incorrect. This describes a finite duration signal

questions.question: How is the fundamental frequency ($f_0$) of a periodic signal related to its period (T)?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Frequency and period are reciprocals of each other
explanations.b: Incorrect. This is not the correct relationship
explanations.c: Correct! The fundamental frequency is the reciprocal of the period
explanations.d: Incorrect. This would give incorrect units

answers.b: A signal that appears periodic but has slightly varying periods
answers.d: A signal that only exists for one period

explanations.a: Incorrect. While quasi-periodic signals don't repeat exactly, they do show regular patterns
explanations.b: Correct! Quasi-periodic signals show periodic-like behavior but with variations in the period
explanations.c: Incorrect. This describes a purely periodic signal

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a basic periodic waveform?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Square wave is a fundamental periodic waveform
explanations.b: Incorrect. Triangular wave is a basic periodic waveform
explanations.c: Correct! A Gaussian pulse is not periodic - it's a single pulse
explanations.d: Incorrect. Full-wave rectified sine is a periodic waveform
